definitions.text: To operate a vehicle: To operate (a wheeled motorized vehicle).
examples: The bridges weren't strong enough to drive (campers) over.
The bridges weren't strong enough to drive (campers) over.

definitions.text: To travel by operating a wheeled motorized vehicle.
examples: I drive to work every day.

definitions.text: Planned, usually long-lasting, effort to achieve something; ability coupled with ambition, determination, and motivation.
examples: Crassus had wealth and wit, but Pompey had drive and Caesar as much again.

definitions.text: Violent or rapid motion; a rushing onward or away; a forced or hurried dispatch of business.
